title: Physical Standards and Medical Requirements for Indian Armed Forces
author: DefenceRoger Editorial
data:
The physical requirements for NDA are non-negotiable and can determine whether a candidate progresses to training.

Key Highlights:
• Minimum height (Male): 157 cm (relaxation for specific categories).
• Minimum height (Female): 152 cm.
• Weight: Must be proportional to height and age as per UPSC/MNS medical standards.
• Recommended running benchmark (Male): 1.6 km under 7 minutes and 2.4 km in 12–15 minutes.
• Selection Process: Written Examination → SSB Interview → Medical Examination → Final Merit List.

Why Physical Requirements Matter:
Many candidates prepare only for Mathematics and GAT while ignoring medical and physical standards. Height, weight, fitness, vision, posture, and overall health are equally important because NDA selects future military officers capable of handling demanding training, field conditions, and operational duties.

Physical Requirements for NDA:
The NDA physical standards include four major areas:
1. Height Standards
2. Weight Standards
3. Functional Fitness & Stamina
4. Medical Fitness including vision and overall health.

Height Requirements:
For Male Candidates:
• Army: Approximately 157 cm.
• Navy: Approximately 157 cm.
• Air Force: Approximately 163 cm.
Candidates must also satisfy body proportion, posture, and skeletal standards.

For Female Candidates:
• Minimum height generally starts around 152 cm depending on service.
• Navy and Air Force may require higher standards.
• Weight must remain proportional to age and height.
• Medical suitability, skeletal alignment, posture, and fitness are carefully assessed.

Female Fitness Considerations:
• Maintain healthy height-weight proportion.
• Good vision, spine, joints, and dental health.
• Avoid issues such as knock knees, flat feet, severe scoliosis, and chronic illness.
• Build cardio, strength, mobility, and endurance rather than focusing only on running.

Weight Standards:
Weight is evaluated according to official height-age tables. Candidates should maintain healthy body composition, avoid obesity or being underweight, and focus on balanced nutrition instead of crash diets or excessive muscle gain.

Fitness Expectations:
Candidates should prepare for:
• Running
• Push-ups
• Sit-ups
• Chin-ups
• Squats
• Rope climbing
• Mobility and flexibility.

Recommended Benchmarks:
• 2.4 km run: 12–15 minutes.
• 20+ Push-ups.
• 30+ Sit-ups.
• 6–8 Chin-ups.
• Daily stretching and mobility work.

Preparation After Class 12:
Male candidates should honestly assess height and weight, improve running endurance, correct posture, reduce body fat naturally, sleep adequately, and begin physical preparation several months before the examination.

Medical Standards:
Medical examination evaluates:
• Vision.
• Hearing.
• Dental health.
• Chest and lungs.
• Heart.
• Spine and skeletal alignment.
• Liver and kidneys.
• Skin conditions.
• Hernia, hydrocele, piles, varicose veins, and other medical conditions.

Vision Standards:
Assessment includes distant vision, near vision, colour vision, binocular vision, refractive errors, and squint. Different services have different eyesight requirements.

Common Medical Rejection Reasons:
• Knock knees.
• Flat feet.
• Severe scoliosis.
• Joint instability.
• Restricted limb movement.
• Chronic asthma.
• Serious allergies.
• Skin disorders.
• Cardiac conditions.
• Neurological disorders.
• Untreated dental disease.

NDA Selection Process:
1. Written Examination.
2. SSB Interview.
3. Medical Examination.
4. Final Merit List.
Each stage evaluates different aspects including academics, officer-like qualities, medical fitness, and vacancy-based merit.

Recommended Daily Routine:
• Run 2–4 km.
• Perform push-ups, squats, sit-ups, planks, and chin-ups.
• Practice mobility exercises.
• Eat balanced meals.
• Study consistently.
• Sleep 7–8 hours.

Common Mistakes:
• Preparing only for written examination.
• Starting fitness too late.
• Ignoring posture and flexibility.
• Following unreliable fitness advice.
• Using unnecessary supplements.
• Hiding medical conditions.

Fitness Tips:
• Train consistently.
• Improve gradually.
• Wear proper running shoes.
• Stretch after workouts.
• Monitor weight weekly.
• Build endurance and discipline together.
